expectations.text: does NOT draft a single bundled ADR titled 'Tenant data architecture'
expectations.evidence: 'I don't want to write a single Tenant data architecture ADR.' No ADR file written.

expectations.text: proposes splitting the bundle into separate ADRs (at least 3 distinct decisions)
expectations.evidence: Proposes 4 separate ADRs (storage substrate, partitioning, access path, backup).

expectations.text: explains why bundling is bad (rationale dilution, hard to supersede independently, or similar)
expectations.evidence: 'Bundling those four sub-decisions into one ADR dilutes the rationale for each, makes it impossible to supersede one without revisiting the others'

expectations.text: asks the user which decision to tackle first
expectations.evidence: 'Decision needed from you: confirm one of: 1. Proceed with the four-ADR split above, starting with 0001-tenant-state-storage-substrate.'

expectations.text: does NOT write a bundled ADR file to disk
